jokes.text: I'm catcher, or I'm nothing!
jokes.setup: Michael is trying to discuss serious legal matters with his imprisoned father
jokes.punchline: George Sr. is having a dramatic meltdown about softball positions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Sr.'s misplaced priorities and dramatic flair even in prison

jokes.text: Your wife's on line one. Michael's wife had been dead for two years.
jokes.setup: Kitty announces Michael's wife is on the phone
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals his wife has been dead for two years
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy setup with perfect timing - the matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: But not immediately.
jokes.setup: Kitty realizes her mistake about Michael's dead wife
jokes.punchline: Narrator notes she didn't realize it right away
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator timing - the pause before her realization makes her obliviousness funnier

jokes.text: If I wanted something your thumb touched, I'd eat the inside of your ear!
jokes.setup: Lucille is complaining about being rejected at the club
jokes.punchline: Bizarre, disgusting insult that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Lucille - a completely illogical but vividly disgusting insult delivered with aristocratic disdain

jokes.text: ? Ta-dah?
jokes.setup: Gob gets stabbed in prison and rushed to hospital
jokes.punchline: He weakly does his magic flourish from a hospital bed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - even after being stabbed, he can't resist his signature move

jokes.text: So I am going after this guy with everything I've got... art. Now we are writing a letter.
jokes.setup: Gob threatens to go after the warden with everything he's got
jokes.punchline: His weapon of choice is... letter writing
jokes.explanation: Great anticlimax - Gob's tough talk deflated by his pathetically mild revenge plan

jokes.text: I wish I would've known you were coming. I am a mess. Don't know if a call from me would've changed that.
jokes.setup: Kitty apologizes for being a mess without warning
jokes.punchline: Gob implies she's always a mess
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based insult that establishes Gob's casual cruelty toward Kitty

jokes.text: Not as much as I like what's underneath it. No, I need your chair.
jokes.setup: Kitty asks if Gob likes her outfit, he seems to be flirting
jokes.punchline: He just wants her chair for his letter writing
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Gob's accidental cruelty and single-minded focus

jokes.text: Can we come? Sorry. It's not for kids.
jokes.setup: Kids ask to come for ice cream
jokes.punchline: It's therapy, but the parents lie saying it's not for kids
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about dysfunctional parenting - they're lying about therapy while creating more need for it

jokes.text: It says 'Bluth Company' right on the side. Plus, it's a stairway. That's gonna catch the eye.
jokes.setup: They're trying to secretly follow parents
jokes.punchline: Their surveillance vehicle is a conspicuous airport stairway with company logo
jokes.explanation: Great visual absurdity - using the most conspicuous vehicle possible for stealth

jokes.text: Also, that House of Pies that went out of business.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains the stairway was a vestige of former wealth
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions they also own a defunct restaurant
jokes.explanation: Classic AD throwaway line that adds to the family's random business failures

jokes.text: he accidentally watched HBO's Oz mistaking it for the classic Judy Garland musical. I'm an innocent man! Eat it, bitch!
jokes.setup: George Michael is terrified of prison
jokes.punchline: He confused the violent HBO show with The Wizard of Oz
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept - child's innocent confusion leading to traumatic misunderstanding, perfectly executed

jokes.text: No. Glasses on. Hair back up. Let's just get that hair right back up.
jokes.setup: Gob asks Kitty to take off glasses and let down hair
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses course when he sees the result
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy setup - the classic movie makeover trope completely backfiring

jokes.text: If I gave a performance that good, I'd have my own Alias type show.
jokes.setup: Lindsay gives emotional confession about their marriage being a lie
jokes.punchline: Tobias thinks it's acting and references a random TV show
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias - completely missing emotional reality and making it about his acting dreams

jokes.text: Without the half-person on the skateboard that grabbed your knee to steady himself.
jokes.setup: Michael compares prison to a carnival
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and disturbing carnival memory
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and unexpected detail that reveals Michael's anxiety through bizarre comparison

jokes.text: Without the half-person on the skateboard that grabbed your knee to steady himself.
jokes.setup: Michael tells George Michael prison is like a carnival
jokes.punchline: Except for this very specific disturbing carnival memory
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and disturbing detail that makes the 'prison as carnival' comparison even more absurd

jokes.text: I'll give you a thousand bucks, you call the next one a strike.
jokes.setup: Prison softball game in 14th inning
jokes.punchline: George Sr. tries to bribe the umpire in prison sports
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Sr. behavior - treating prison softball with the same corruption as business

jokes.text: I'm gonna die in here. They're gonna kill Grandpa.
jokes.setup: George Michael asks how prison is
jokes.punchline: George Sr. immediately terrifies him with death talk
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of inappropriate oversharing with a child, typical George Sr. selfishness

jokes.text: Give Pop-Pop your hair! No touching! No touching! No touching!
jokes.setup: George Sr. asks for George Michael's hair for escape plan
jokes.punchline: George Michael's repeated 'no touching' protest
jokes.explanation: Great escalation using George Michael's established catchphrase in new context

jokes.text: No batter. No batter. No batter.
jokes.setup: Prison softball continues in background
jokes.punchline: Inmates doing little league chants in maximum security
jokes.explanation: Solid background absurdity - hardened criminals acting like little leaguers

jokes.text: I don't totally get the hair joke, but I'm fine, Dad.
jokes.setup: George Michael was just traumatized by his grandfather
jokes.punchline: He thinks it was just a weird joke
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing George Michael's innocence and effort to stay positive

jokes.text: You remember the carjacker? - Aaah! - Well, she's certainly toughened up since then.
jokes.setup: George Sr. says Lucille is easily threatened
jokes.punchline: Flashback to her screaming, then cut to her current toughness
jokes.explanation: Good character development humor showing Lucille's evolution through flashback contrast

jokes.text: Daddy horny, Michael.
jokes.setup: George Sr. is getting emotional about missing human contact
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to crude, caveman-like announcement
jokes.explanation: Instant classic - perfectly captures George Sr.'s inappropriate oversharing with maximum cringe

jokes.text: You've been here two months. It's hard to gauge time.
jokes.setup: George Sr. says he hasn't had sex in a month
jokes.punchline: He's been in prison for two months, can't gauge time
jokes.explanation: Good math-based comedy revealing George Sr.'s delusions about his situation

jokes.text: Pool food. My system's not used to curly fries.
jokes.setup: Lucille makes disgusted sound
jokes.punchline: Blames it on eating common pool food like curly fries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - treating pool food like exotic cuisine that upsets her delicate system

jokes.text: If you're going to say 'pride,' you're wrong. Wasn't gonna say pride, Mom.
jokes.setup: Michael struggles to ask his mom to visit dad intimately
jokes.punchline: Lucille misunderstands what he's building up to
jokes.explanation: Good awkwardness as Michael tries to navigate the uncomfortable conversation

jokes.text: I think that you should... visit him... in an... intimate... marital... trailer.
jokes.setup: Michael struggles to ask his mother to have conjugal visits
jokes.punchline: He forces out the awkward request word by painful word
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Michael's discomfort is palpable as he forces himself through this request

jokes.text: Daddy horny, Michael. He said some wonderful things.
jokes.setup: Lucille asks if George Sr. said wonderful things about missing her
jokes.punchline: Cut to flashback of 'Daddy horny' - Michael calls it wonderful
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and ironic recontextualization - the crude line becomes 'wonderful'

jokes.text: 'Golf membership' want it?
jokes.setup: Michael asks if she really wants to visit George Sr.
jokes.punchline: She measures her desire in terms of golf membership benefits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - measuring spousal duty against country club privileges

jokes.text: And these, Tobias. When was the last time you looked at these?
jokes.setup: Lindsay is role-playing as a frustrated woman
jokes.punchline: Presumably gesturing at her body parts during therapy
jokes.explanation: Awkward therapy moment with implied visual comedy of inappropriate exposure

jokes.text: Right! If I'm no Schwimmer, you're no Jennifer Aniston! Maybe I'm not even Lisa Kudrow.
jokes.setup: Therapy exercise continues
jokes.punchline: Tobias starts ranking himself among Friends cast members
jokes.explanation: Pure Tobias - completely derailing therapy with random actor comparisons and self-deprecation

jokes.text: Now there are two men I wanna leave.
jokes.setup: Therapy was supposed to help their marriage
jokes.punchline: Now Lindsay wants to leave both Tobias and the therapist
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - therapy making things worse and adding another target

jokes.text: I just came from convincing Mom to have sex with Dad. God! What is wrong with you? That is disgusting!
jokes.setup: Michael announces his 'good news' proudly
jokes.punchline: Gob is horrified by the inappropriate news
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal - usually Michael is the one appalled by Gob's behavior

jokes.text: I have a sense of propriety. All right?
jokes.setup: Gob claims to have standards
jokes.punchline: This from the man who just seduced their father's secretary
jokes.explanation: Good irony given Gob's complete lack of propriety in every other situation

jokes.text: I'm gonna use the touch lamp to set the mood in the conjugal trailer when Dad's nailing Mom!
jokes.setup: Michael threatens to take away Gob's lamp
jokes.punchline: Describes in horrible detail what the lamp will be used for
jokes.explanation: Michael's uncharacteristic crudeness shows how the situation has broken him

jokes.text: I'm not saying 'I don't care' like kids who say they don't when they do... 'cause I don't.
jokes.setup: Maeby claims not to care about her parents' divorce
jokes.punchline: Over-explains her indifference in a way that reveals she does care
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Maeby's defensive intelligence and emotional protection

jokes.text: Sometimes I don't know if it's gonna be a long or short hug or a middle-medium hug. It's hard to tell sometimes.
jokes.setup: George Michael and Maeby have an awkward hug
jokes.punchline: He over-analyzes the hug mechanics
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael awkwardness - overthinking simple social interaction

jokes.text: The drive was a little awkward.
jokes.setup: George Sr. asks if Michael was uncomfortable bringing Lucille
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about driving his mother to prison sex
jokes.explanation: Classic understated reaction to an obviously traumatic experience

jokes.text: Every Friday for the last eight years. Daddy... Horny. I got that.
jokes.setup: Michael asks when the affair started
jokes.punchline: It's been going on for years, callback to earlier line
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the affair's long duration plus callback to signature line

jokes.text: I'm not going to have sex with you, George. I came to tell you how much I hate you for what you've done to this family.
jokes.setup: Expected conjugal visit scene
jokes.punchline: Lucille came to verbally abuse George Sr. instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille move - using the conjugal trailer for emotional warfare instead of intimacy

jokes.text: And you're as powerful as a bear. My husband the bear. This bear needs some honey, wife.
jokes.setup: Lucille and George Sr. reconciling in trailer
jokes.punchline: Bizarre bear/honey sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Hilariously bizarre sexual dialogue that's both absurd and deeply uncomfortable

jokes.text: Have we done up, off? Wrong and bright. That's be... brighter!
jokes.setup: Gob trying different combinations of glasses/hair with Kitty
jokes.punchline: Keeps making lighting worse while stalling
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy sequence showing Gob's desperate stalling tactics

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't 'Sincerely Yours.'
jokes.setup: Warden encounters Gob after reading his letter
jokes.punchline: Mocks him by referencing his formal letter closing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Gob's pathetic letter-writing threat, shows it backfired

jokes.text: Oh, God, Mommy! What did you do?
jokes.setup: Gob is pressed against trailer window
jokes.punchline: Forced to witness his parents' intimate reunion
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy payoff - Gob's earlier propriety coming back to traumatize him

jokes.text: Oh, God, Mommy! What did you do?
jokes.setup: Gob pressed against trailer window
jokes.punchline: Forced to witness parents' intimate reunion
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy setup with Gob's traumatic witness to parental intimacy.

jokes.text: 'Cause I got news... he's, uh... he's guilty. Oh, yeah. Incredibly guilty.
jokes.setup: George Michael worried about innocent people in prison
jokes.punchline: Michael emphatically confirms George Sr.'s guilt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's honesty and the escalating 'incredibly' adds punch

jokes.text: I've decided that... I want to become an actor too.
jokes.setup: Lindsay was just traumatized by therapy with an actor
jokes.punchline: Decides to become an actor herself
jokes.explanation: Classic Lindsay - making the worst possible decision based on recent trauma

jokes.text: There's a good chance that I... that I may... have committed some, um... light... treason.
jokes.setup: George Sr. finally admits to his crimes
jokes.punchline: Describes treason as 'light' like it's a minor offense
jokes.explanation: Instant classic line - 'light treason' became a cultural reference, perfect character comedy
